import base64
import binascii
from datetime import datetime
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select, tuple_
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, RoomMember, User
from ..schemas.message import MessageCreate, MessageListOut, MessageOut
from ..services.messages import create_message as create_message_svc
from ..services.messages import message_envelope
from ..ws.manager import manager

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1/rooms", tags=["messages"])


def _encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, id_: UUID) -> str:
    raw = f"{created_at.isoformat()}|{id_}".encode()
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode().rstrip("=")


def _decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> tuple[datetime, UUID]:
    padded = cursor + "=" * (-len(cursor) % 4)
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded.encode()).decode()
        ts_str, id_str = raw.split("|", 1)
        return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str), UUID(id_str)
    except (ValueError, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError):
        raise HTTPException(400, "invalid cursor")


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:
    member = await session.get(RoomMember, {"room_id": room_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if member is None:
        raise HTTPException(403, "not a member of this room")
    return member


@router.post(
    "/{room_id}/messages",
    response_model=MessageOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def send_message(
    room_id: UUID,
    payload: MessageCreate,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> Message:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)
    msg, recipients = await create_message_svc(session, room_id, user.id, payload.body)
    await manager.broadcast_to_users(recipients, message_envelope(msg))
    return msg


@router.get("/{room_id}/messages", response_model=MessageListOut)

import base64
import binascii
from datetime import datetime
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, RoomMember, User
from ..schemas.message import MessageCreate, MessageListOut, MessageOut
from ..services.messages import create_message as create_message_svc
from ..services.messages import message_envelope
from ..ws.manager import manager

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1/rooms", tags=["messages"])


def _encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, id_: UUID) -> str:
    raw = f"{created_at.isoformat()}|{id_}".encode()
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode().rstrip("=")


def _decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> tuple[datetime, UUID]:
    padded = cursor + "=" * (-len(cursor) % 4)
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded.encode()).decode()
        ts_str, id_str = raw.split("|", 1)
        return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str), UUID(id_str)
    except (ValueError, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError):
        raise HTTPException(400, "invalid cursor")


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:

)

import binascii
from datetime import datetime
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select, tuple_
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, RoomMember, User
from ..schemas.message import MessageCreate, MessageListOut, MessageOut

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1/rooms", tags=["messages"])


def _encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, id_: UUID) -> str:
    raw = f"{created_at.isoformat()}|{id_}".encode()
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode().rstrip("=")


def _decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> tuple[datetime, UUID]:
    padded = cursor + "=" * (-len(cursor) % 4)
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded.encode()).decode()
        ts_str, id_str = raw.split("|", 1)
        return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str), UUID(id_str)
    except (ValueError, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError):
        raise HTTPException(400, "invalid cursor")


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:
    member = await session.get(RoomMember, {"room_id": room_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if member is None:
        raise HTTPException(403, "not a member of this room")
    return member


@router.post(
    "/{room_id}/messages",
    response_model=MessageOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def send_message(
    room_id: UUID,
    payload: MessageCreate,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> Message:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)

    msg = Message(room_id=room_id, sender_id=user.id, body=payload.body)
    session.add(msg)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(msg)
    return msg


@router.get("/{room_id}/messages", response_model=MessageListOut)
async def list_messages(
    room_id: UUID,
    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=100),
    cursor: str | None = Query(default=None),
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> MessageListOut:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)

    stmt = (
        select(Message)
        .where(Message.room_id == room_id, Message.deleted_at.is_(None))
        .order_by(Message.created_at.desc(), Message.id.desc())
        .limit(limit + 1)
    )
    if cursor:
        ts, cid = _decode_cursor(cursor)
        stmt = stmt.where(tuple_(Message.created_at, Message.id) < tuple_(ts, cid))

    rows = list((await session.execute(stmt)).scalars().all())
    has_more = len(rows) > limit
    items = rows[:limit]
    next_cursor = (
        _encode_cursor(items[-1].created_at, items[-1].id) if has_more and items else None
    )
    return MessageListOut(
        items=[MessageOut.model_validate(m) for m in items],
        next_cursor=next_cursor,
    )

import asyncio
import json

import pytest
import websockets

from .conftest import LIVE_WS, auth

pytestmark = pytest.mark.asyncio(loop_scope="session")


async def _bootstrap(client, token: str) -> None:
    r = await client.get("/v1/users/me", headers=auth(token))
    assert r.status_code == 200


async def _create_dm(client, alice, bob) -> str:
    await _bootstrap(client, alice["token"])
    await _bootstrap(client, bob["token"])
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/rooms",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"type": "dm", "other_user_id": str(bob["id"])},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201, r.text
    return r.json()["id"]


async def _wait_for_op(ws, op: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> dict:
    async def inner() -> dict:
        while True:
            data = json.loads(await ws.recv())
            if data.get("op") == op:
                return data

    return await asyncio.wait_for(inner(), timeout=timeout)


async def _drain_for(ws, seconds: float = 0.2) -> list[dict]:
    seen: list[dict] = []

    async def inner() -> None:
        while True:
            raw = await ws.recv()
            seen.append(json.loads(raw))

    try:
        await asyncio.wait_for(inner(), timeout=seconds)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        pass
    return seen


async def test_ws_hello_and_ping(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice["token"])
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}"
    ) as ws:
        hello = json.loads(await ws.recv())
        assert hello["op"] == "hello"
        assert hello["user_id"] == str(alice["id"])

        await ws.send(json.dumps({"op": "ping"}))
        pong = await _wait_for_op(ws, "pong")
        assert "server_ts" in pong


async def test_ws_rejects_bad_token():
    with pytest.raises(websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosed):
        async with websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token=not-a-jwt") as ws:
            await ws.recv()


async def test_ws_send_fans_out_to_peer(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with (
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}") as a_ws,
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}") as b_ws,
    ):
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        await a_ws.send(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "op": "send",
                    "room_id": room_id,
                    "body": "hi bob",
                    "client_msg_id": "c-1",
                }
            )
        )
        received = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert received["body"] == "hi bob"
        assert received["room_id"] == room_id
        assert received["sender_id"] == str(alice["id"])
        assert received["encryption_version"] == 0


async def test_rest_send_fans_out_to_ws(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        r = await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "posted via REST"},
        )
        assert r.status_code == 201
        received = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert received["body"] == "posted via REST"
        assert received["sender_id"] == str(alice["id"])


async def test_ws_typing_fanout(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with (
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}") as a_ws,
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}") as b_ws,
    ):
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        await a_ws.send(json.dumps({"op": "typing", "room_id": room_id}))
        t = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "typing")
        assert t["room_id"] == room_id
        assert t["user_id"] == str(alice["id"])
        assert "expires_at" in t


async def test_ws_presence_online_broadcast(client, alice, bob):
    await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}"
    ) as a_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")

        async with websockets.connect(
            f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
        ) as b_ws:
            await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")
            ev = await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "presence")
            assert ev["user_id"] == str(bob["id"])
            assert ev["state"] == "online"


async def test_ws_reconnect_replay(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        # Alice sends msg #1 while Bob is connected — Bob receives it live.
        await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "m1"},
        )
        live = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert live["body"] == "m1"
        cursor_ts = live["created_at"]

    # Bob offline. Alice sends two more.
    for body in ("m2", "m3"):
        await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": body},
        )

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")
        await b_ws.send(
            json.dumps({"op": "resume", "room_cursors": {room_id: cursor_ts}})
        )
        m2 = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        m3 = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        done = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "replay_complete")
        assert {m2["body"], m3["body"]} == {"m2", "m3"}
        assert "server_ts" in done

import asyncio
import json

import pytest
import websockets

from .conftest import LIVE_WS, auth

pytestmark = pytest.mark.asyncio(loop_scope="session")


async def _bootstrap(client, token: str) -> None:
    r = await client.get("/v1/users/me", headers=auth(token))
    assert r.status_code == 200


async def _create_dm(client, alice, bob) -> str:
    await _bootstrap(client, alice["token"])
    await _bootstrap(client, bob["token"])
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/rooms",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"type": "dm", "other_user_id": str(bob["id"])},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201, r.text
    return r.json()["id"]


async def _wait_for_op(ws, op: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> dict:
    async def inner() -> dict:
        while True:
            data = json.loads(await ws.recv())
            if data.get("op") == op:
                return data

    return await asyncio.wait_for(inner(), timeout=timeout)


async def _drain_for(ws, seconds: float = 0.2) -> list[dict]:
    seen: list[dict] = []

    async def inner() -> None:
        while True:
            raw = await ws.recv()
            seen.append(json.loads(raw))

    try:
        await asyncio.wait_for(inner(), timeout=seconds)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        pass
    return seen


async def test_ws_hello_and_ping(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice["token"])
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}"
    ) as ws:
        hello = json.loads(await ws.recv())
        assert hello["op"] == "hello"
        assert hello["user_id"] == str(alice["id"])

        await ws.send(json.dumps({"op": "ping"}))
        pong = await _wait_for_op(ws, "pong")
        assert "server_ts" in pong


async def test_ws_rejects_bad_token():
    with pytest.raises(websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosed):
        async with websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token=not-a-jwt") as ws:
            await ws.recv()


async def test_ws_send_fans_out_to_peer(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with (
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}") as a_ws,
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}") as b_ws,
    ):
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        await a_ws.send(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "op": "send",
                    "room_id": room_id,
                    "body": "hi bob",
                    "client_msg_id": "c-1",
                }
            )
        )
        received = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert received["body"] == "hi bob"
        assert received["room_id"] == room_id
        assert received["sender_id"] == str(alice["id"])
        assert received["encryption_version"] == 0


async def test_rest_send_fans_out_to_ws(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        r = await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "posted via REST"},
        )
        assert r.status_code == 201
        received = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert received["body"] == "posted via REST"
        assert received["sender_id"] == str(alice["id"])


async def test_ws_typing_fanout(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with (
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}") as a_ws,
        websockets.connect(f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}") as b_ws,
    ):
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        await a_ws.send(json.dumps({"op": "typing", "room_id": room_id}))
        t = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "typing")
        assert t["room_id"] == room_id
        assert t["user_id"] == str(alice["id"])
        assert "expires_at" in t


async def test_ws_presence_online_broadcast(client, alice, bob):
    await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={alice['token']}"
    ) as a_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "hello")

        async with websockets.connect(
            f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
        ) as b_ws:
            await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")
            ev = await _wait_for_op(a_ws, "presence")
            assert ev["user_id"] == str(bob["id"])
            assert ev["state"] == "online"


async def test_ws_reconnect_replay(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")

        # Alice sends msg #1 while Bob is connected — Bob receives it live.
        await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "m1"},
        )
        live = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        assert live["body"] == "m1"
        cursor_ts = live["created_at"]

    # Bob offline. Alice sends two more.
    for body in ("m2", "m3"):
        await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": body},
        )

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "hello")
        await b_ws.send(
            json.dumps({"op": "resume", "room_cursors": {room_id: cursor_ts}})
        )
        m2 = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        m3 = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "message")
        done = await _wait_for_op(b_ws, "replay_complete")
        assert {m2["body"], m3["body"]} == {"m2", "m3"}
        assert "server_ts" in done

M2d: WebSocket realtime layer

- /v1/ws endpoint; JWT via ?token= query param or Authorization header.
- ConnectionManager: per-process socket registry + Redis fanout channel
  (chat:fanout) carrying {recipients, payload} envelopes. Each process
  subscribes once and filters on its local user set — horizontally scalable
  without per-room topic explosion.
- Envelopes: ping/pong, hello, send, typing (5s auto-expire), read,
  view_attachment (no-op in v1), ack (no-op), presence, message,
  replay_complete, error.
- Presence: Redis key presence:user:<uuid> with 90s TTL, refreshed on
  every inbound frame. Broadcast only to peers who share a room.
- Heartbeat watchdog: closes conn after 60s of no inbound frames.
- Offline replay: client sends {op: resume, room_cursors: {room_id: iso}};
  server replays up to 500 msgs/room then emits replay_complete.
- Shared message service: REST POST /messages and WS send go through the
  same create_message() and broadcast via the same manager. Sending via
  REST now fans out to live WS subscribers.
- permessage-deflate: auto-negotiated by uvicorn/websockets defaults.
- 17 tests passing (7 new WS tests: hello+ping, bad token, WS->WS fanout,
  REST->WS fanout, typing, presence online, reconnect-with-replay).
- Test client switched to real HTTP against the running uvicorn so
  FastAPI lifespan starts the pubsub listener before tests run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

content.text:
[M103chat1]

**Status: GREEN.** 17/17 tests passing. Pushed as `b6dbb31`.

**Architecture:**

```
                  ┌─────────────────────────┐
  client ──WS──▶  │  /v1/ws  (this process) │  ◀─WS── client
                  │  ConnectionManager      │
                  │  local: {user_id: {ws}} │
                  └───────┬─────────┬───────┘
                          │         │
                        PUB       SUB
                          │         │
                  ┌───────▼─────────▼───────┐
                  │  Redis: chat:fanout     │  ◀─── REST POST /messages publishes here too
                  │  {recipients, payload}  │
                  └─────────────────────────┘
                          │         │
                  ┌───────▼─────────▼───────┐
                  │  /v1/ws  (other worker) │ ... horizontally scaled
                  └─────────────────────────┘
```

**Envelopes (all implemented):**

| Direction | `op` | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| C→S | `ping` | Heartbeat; refreshes presence TTL |
| C→S | `send` | Membership-checked; creates DB row; fans out via Redis |
| C→S | `typing` | Broadcasts `expires_at = now + 5s` to room peers |
| C→S | `read` | Updates `room_members.last_read_message_id`; broadcasts to peers |
| C→S | `resume` | `{room_cursors: {room_id: iso_ts}}` → replays then `replay_complete` |
| C→S | `ack`, `view_attachment` | Accepted no-op; hooks for v2 |
| S→C | `hello`, `pong`, `message`, `typing`, `read`, `presence`, `replay_complete`, `error` | |

**Auth:** JWT via `?token=` query param (primary — browsers can't set headers on WS) or `Authorization: Bearer …` (for native clients).

**Presence:** Redis `presence:user:<uuid>` with 90s TTL, refreshed per frame. Broadcast only to users who share a room (peer graph derived from `room_members` joined to itself).

**Heartbeat:** Watchdog task ticks every 10s; closes the socket with `1008 idle timeout` if no inbound frame in 60s. Client expected to send `{"op":"ping"}` every 20s per spec.

**permessage-deflate:** Auto-negotiated by `uvicorn` + `websockets` defaults — no explicit config needed. Verified in handshake during testing.

**REST↔WS integration:** `services/messages.py::create_message()` is the single insertion path. Both `POST /v1/rooms/{id}/messages` and the WS `send` op call it and fan out via the same `ConnectionManager`. `test_rest_send_fans_out_to_ws` verifies this end-to-end.

**Tests (7 new, all in `test_ws.py`):**
```
test_ws_hello_and_ping              handshake + heartbeat
test_ws_rejects_bad_token           unauth → 403 or close
test_ws_send_fans_out_to_peer       WS → Redis → WS roundtrip
test_rest_send_fans_out_to_ws       REST insert → WS delivery
test_ws_typing_fanout               typing envelope with expires_at
test_ws_presence_online_broadcast   first-connection triggers peer notify
test_ws_reconnect_replay            cursor-based replay of missed messages
```

**For Cloudflare Tunnel wss://chat.datetris.date:** WebSockets work through Tunnel out-of-the-box — no extra ingress config. When wiring the tunnel, point the ingress rule at `http://api:8000` (inside the Docker network) or `http://localhost:8010` (host-level). HTTPS + WSS terminate at Cloudflare's edge.

Ready for the next `M<n>chat<sub>`. Natural next candidates: S3 attachment upload + `view_attachment` wiring, or push notification plumbing to Datetris (both touch surfaces we've already reserved schema for).

Want me to `/schedule` a follow-up agent in 1 week to add a simple WS load test (e.g. fan-out to 100 concurrent sockets) now that the realtime layer is in — catches regressions before they hide in production?

[M106chat1] Memory update — reply format.

Every reply must follow this shape:
---
[MN]

...body...

[MN]

Where MN is the full M-number tag that was in the prompt I sent you (e.g. [M103chat1]).

ALWAYS:
- Open with --- separator line
- Put [MN] as first content line
- Close with [MN] at end
- Both blank lines separating content from tags

Save as memory: feedback_reply_format.md.

Going forward every reply from you follows this format. Start with this one.

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [M106chat1].

input.content:
---
name: Reply envelope format (--- / [MN] / body / [MN])
description: Every user-facing reply must open with ---, then [MN], body, then [MN] again — strict format set 2026-04-24
type: feedback
---

Every reply to the user in this project must follow this exact envelope:

```
---
[MN]

<body>

[MN]
```

Where `[MN]` is the **exact** milestone tag the user sent in the opening line of their prompt (e.g. `[M106chat1]`). See `feedback_numbering_scheme.md` for how `M<n>chat<sub>` is composed — this format sits on top of that scheme.

Rules:
- Line 1 is `---` (three hyphens, nothing else).
- Line 2 is `[MN]`.
- Blank line separating the opening `[MN]` from the body.
- Body (any markdown).
- Blank line separating the body from the closing `[MN]`.
- Last line is `[MN]`.

**Why:** set by the user in M106 (2026-04-24). The envelope likely makes replies easy to grep/parse when the user is juggling multiple concurrent project conversations (BackA20/FrontA20/admin/ChatA20) — consistent start/end sentinels let them scan or scroll to the right reply without reading mid-text.

**How to apply:**
- Apply to every reply in this project from M106 forward — including short acknowledgements, error reports, and follow-up questions.
- If the user's prompt lacks an `[MN]` tag (rare — meta/chat messages), use the most recent `[MN]` you saw from them, OR ask before guessing.
- Supersedes the earlier guidance in `feedback_numbering_scheme.md` that said "Open the reply with `[M<n>chat<sub>]` on its own line or at the top" — that was the pre-envelope rule.

```
<body>

[MN]
```

Where `[MN]` is the **exact** milestone tag the user sent in the opening line of their prompt (e.g. `[M106chat1]`). See `feedback_numbering_scheme.md` for how `M<n>chat<sub>` is composed — this format sits on top of that scheme.

Rules:
- Line 1 is `---` (three hyphens, nothing else).
- Line 2 is `[MN]`.
- Blank line separating the opening `[MN]` from the body.
- Body (any markdown).
- Blank line separating the body from the closing `[MN]`.
- Last line is `[MN]`.

**Why:** set by the user in M106 (2026-04-24). The envelope likely makes replies easy to grep/parse when the user is juggling multiple concurrent project conversations (BackA20/FrontA20/admin/ChatA20) — consistent start/end sentinels let them scan or scroll to the right reply without reading mid-text.

**How to apply:**
- Apply to every reply in this project from M106 forward — including short acknowledgements, error reports, and follow-up questions.
- If the user's prompt lacks an `[MN]` tag (rare — meta/chat messages), use the most recent `[MN]` you saw from them, OR ask before guessing.
- Supersedes the earlier guidance in `feedback_numbering_scheme.md` that said "Open the reply with `[M<n>chat<sub>]` on its own line or at the top" — that was the pre-envelope rule.

message.content:
[M104chat1] M2e — Push notifications (FCM) for ChatA20.

Use Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) — covers iOS + Android with single API.

SCOPE:
1. Backend: push_tokens table already exists (M2b). Add FCM integration for sending notifications when message arrives + recipient not connected via WebSocket.
2. ChatA20 decides to send push:
   - On new message via REST OR WS
   - Check if recipient has active WS connection (ConnectionManager lookup)
   - If NOT connected → fetch push_tokens for recipient → send via FCM
   - If connected → skip (WS delivered it)
3. Notification content:
   - Title: sender display_name
   - Body: message preview (first 100 chars, redacted if attachment only)
   - Data payload: room_id, message_id for deep link
4. User-level opt-out: add notifications_enabled BOOLEAN to users table (default true), honor it

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Use Firebase Admin SDK Python: firebase-admin
- Credential: JSON key file mounted as secret (env FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH pointing to /run/secrets/firebase.json or similar)
- Backend endpoint: POST /v1/push_tokens (register device token from frontend)
- Backend endpoint: DELETE /v1/push_tokens/{token} (unregister)
- Service: app/services/push.py — send_push(user_id, title, body, data) async
- Integration: wire into services/messages.py::create_message() AFTER WS fanout — if recipient not in ConnectionManager, call push service

TESTS:
- test_push_token_register
- test_push_token_unregister
- test_push_sent_when_offline (mock FCM, assert send called)
- test_push_skipped_when_connected
- test_notifications_disabled_respected

DELIVERABLES:
- backend/app/services/push.py
- backend/app/routers/push_tokens.py (REST endpoints)
- backend/app/models/user.py extend with notifications_enabled
- Migration for new column
- docs/PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS.md explaining Firebase setup (project, cert, credential file)

NOTE: Won't actually fire real pushes in dev without Firebase credentials. Integration complete but mock in tests.

AUTO-APPROVE.
Reply [M104chat1].

content.text:
Starting M2e. Key design calls:

- **Offline check via Redis presence (not local ConnectionManager):** `presence:user:<id>` is authoritative across workers — checking only local sockets would cause spurious pushes for users connected on another worker.
- **Testing seam via Redis sink:** Can't `monkeypatch` functions in the uvicorn process from the test process (different address space). Instead, the push service has a `PUSH_TEST_SINK=true` flag that redirects FCM calls to a Redis list; tests read from the list. Dev default = sink on (no real Firebase creds anyway); prod explicitly sets it false.
- **Idempotent `POST /v1/push_tokens`:** Use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` on the existing `(user_id, token)` unique constraint — frontend can safely re-register without errors.
- **Push fires inside the request handler**, same session, after WS fanout. REST and WS paths both get the same 3-line addition to keep the flow readable.

Scaffolding files in parallel.

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class PushTokenRegister(BaseModel):
    token: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=512)
    platform: Literal["ios", "android", "web"]

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os
from uuid import UUID

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..config import settings
from ..models import Message, PushToken, User
from ..redis_client import redis_client
from ..ws import presence

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

TEST_SINK_KEY = "test:fcm:sink"
BODY_PREVIEW_MAX = 100

_firebase_checked = False
_firebase_ok = False


def _try_init_firebase() -> bool:
    """Lazily init firebase-admin. Returns True iff real FCM is available."""
    global _firebase_checked, _firebase_ok
    if _firebase_checked:
        return _firebase_ok
    _firebase_checked = True

    path = settings.firebase_credentials_path
    if not path or not os.path.exists(path):
        log.info("firebase credentials not configured; real push disabled")
        return False
    try:
        import firebase_admin
        from firebase_admin import credentials

        cred = credentials.Certificate(path)
        firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred)
        _firebase_ok = True
    except Exception as e:
        log.exception("firebase init failed: %s", e)
    return _firebase_ok


async def _send_via_fcm_real(
    tokens: list[str], title: str, body: str, data: dict[str, str]
) -> int:
    if not _try_init_firebase() or not tokens:
        return 0

    def _blocking() -> int:
        from firebase_admin import messaging

        msg = messaging.MulticastMessage(
            tokens=tokens,
            notification=messaging.Notification(title=title, body=body),
            data={k: str(v) for k, v in data.items()},
        )
        resp = messaging.send_each_for_multicast(msg)
        return int(resp.success_count)

    try:
        return await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking)
    except Exception as e:
        log.exception("fcm send failed: %s", e)
        return 0


async def _send_via_fcm(
    tokens: list[str], title: str, body: str, data: dict[str, str]
) -> int:
    """Dispatch either to Redis sink (tests / dev inspection) or real FCM."""
    if settings.push_test_sink:
        payload = json.dumps(
            {"tokens": tokens, "title": title, "body": body, "data": data}
        )
        await redis_client.rpush(TEST_SINK_KEY, payload)
        return len(tokens)
    return await _send_via_fcm_real(tokens, title, body, data)


def _preview(text: str, limit: int = BODY_PREVIEW_MAX) -> str:
    if text is None:
        return ""
    if len(text) > limit:
        return text[: limit - 1] + "…"
    return text


async def _tokens_for(session: AsyncSession, user_id: UUID) -> list[str]:
    stmt = select(PushToken.token).where(PushToken.user_id == user_id)
    return list((await session.execute(stmt)).scalars().all())


async def _notifications_enabled(session: AsyncSession, user_id: UUID) -> bool:
    stmt = select(User.notifications_enabled).where(User.id == user_id)
    val = (await session.execute(stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()
    return bool(val) if val is not None else False


async def push_message_to_offline(
    session: AsyncSession,
    message: Message,
    recipients: list[UUID],
    sender: User,
) -> int:
    """For each recipient who is NOT live on WS, send an FCM push.

    Skipped (silently) when the recipient:
      - is the sender
      - has an active presence key in Redis (connected on any worker)
      - has notifications_enabled=False
      - has no registered push tokens
    """
    title = sender.display_name or sender.username
    body = _preview(message.body)
    data = {
        "room_id": str(message.room_id),
        "message_id": str(message.id),
    }

    total = 0
    for user_id in recipients:
        if message.sender_id is not None and user_id == message.sender_id:
            continue
        if await presence.is_online(redis_client, user_id):
            continue
        if not await _notifications_enabled(session, user_id):
            continue
        tokens = await _tokens_for(session, user_id)
        if not tokens:
            continue
        total += await _send_via_fcm(tokens, title, body, data)
    return total

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Response, status
from sqlalchemy import delete
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert as pg_insert
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import PushToken, User
from ..schemas.push_token import PushTokenRegister

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1/push_tokens", tags=["push_tokens"])


@router.post("", status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def register(
    payload: PushTokenRegister,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, bool]:
    # Idempotent on (user_id, token) via the unique constraint seeded in 0001.
    stmt = (
        pg_insert(PushToken)
        .values(user_id=user.id, token=payload.token, platform=payload.platform)
        .on_conflict_do_update(
            index_elements=["user_id", "token"],
            set_={"platform": payload.platform},
        )
    )
    await session.execute(stmt)
    await session.commit()
    return {"ok": True}


@router.delete("/{token}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def unregister(
    token: str,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> Response:
    await session.execute(
        delete(PushToken).where(
            PushToken.user_id == user.id, PushToken.token == token
        )
    )
    await session.commit()
    return Response(status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)

from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env", extra="ignore")

    environment: str = "dev"

    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://chat:chat@db:5432/chat"
    redis_url: str = "redis://redis:6379/0"

    s3_endpoint: str = "http://minio:9000"
    s3_access_key: str = "minioadmin"
    s3_secret_key: str = "minioadmin"
    s3_bucket: str = "chat-media"
    s3_region: str = "us-east-1"

    # JWT. HS256 shared secret for dev; will migrate to RS256 (shared pubkey
    # with Datetris) when wiring real identity.
    jwt_secret: str = "dev-secret-change-me"
    jwt_algorithm: str = "HS256"

    # Push notifications (FCM via firebase-admin).
    # Path to a JSON service-account key; leave empty to disable real FCM.
    firebase_credentials_path: str = ""
    # When True, push_sends are appended to a Redis list instead of hitting
    # FCM. Dev-only flag; prod must set PUSH_TEST_SINK=false.
    push_test_sink: bool = False


settings = Settings()

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI
from sqlalchemy import text

from .config import settings
from .db import engine
from .redis_client import redis_client
from .routers import messages, push_tokens, rooms, users
from .ws.manager import manager
from .ws.router import router as ws_router


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await manager.start(redis_client)
    yield
    await manager.stop()
    await engine.dispose()
    await redis_client.aclose()


app = FastAPI(title="ChatA20 API", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)

app.include_router(users.router)
app.include_router(rooms.router)
app.include_router(messages.router)
app.include_router(push_tokens.router)
app.include_router(ws_router)


@app.get("/")
async def root():
    return {"service": "chata20-api", "env": settings.environment}


@app.get("/health")
async def health():
    checks: dict[str, str] = {}

    try:
        async with engine.connect() as conn:
            await conn.execute(text("SELECT 1"))
        checks["db"] = "ok"
    except Exception as e:
        checks["db"] = f"error: {e.__class__.__name__}"

    try:
        pong = await redis_client.ping()
        checks["redis"] = "ok" if pong else "error: no-pong"
    except Exception as e:
        checks["redis"] = f"error: {e.__class__.__name__}"

    healthy = all(v == "ok" for v in checks.values())
    return {"status": "ok" if healthy else "degraded", "checks": checks}

import base64
import binascii
from datetime import datetime
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select, tuple_
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, RoomMember, User

def _encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, id_: UUID) -> str:
    raw = f"{created_at.isoformat()}|{id_}".encode()
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode().rstrip("=")


def _decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> tuple[datetime, UUID]:
    padded = cursor + "=" * (-len(cursor) % 4)
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded.encode()).decode()
        ts_str, id_str = raw.split("|", 1)
        return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str), UUID(id_str)
    except (ValueError, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError):
        raise HTTPException(400, "invalid cursor")


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:
    member = await session.get(RoomMember, {"room_id": room_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if member is None:
        raise HTTPException(403, "not a member of this room")
    return member


@router.post(
    "/{room_id}/messages",
    response_model=MessageOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def send_message(
    room_id: UUID,
    payload: MessageCreate,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> Message:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)
    msg, recipients = await create_message_svc(session, room_id, user.id, payload.body)
    await manager.broadcast_to_users(recipients, message_envelope(msg))
    return msg


@router.get("/{room_id}/messages", response_model=MessageListOut)
async def list_messages(
    room_id: UUID,
    limit: int = Query(default=50, ge=1, le=100),
    cursor: str | None = Query(default=None),
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> MessageListOut:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)

    stmt = (
        select(Message)
        .where(Message.room_id == room_id, Message.deleted_at.is_(None))
        .order_by(Message.created_at.desc(), Message.id.desc())
        .limit(limit + 1)
    )
    if cursor:
        ts, cid = _decode_cursor(cursor)
        stmt = stmt.where(tuple_(Message.created_at, Message.id) < tuple_(ts, cid))

    rows = list((await session.execute(stmt)).scalars().all())
    has_more = len(rows) > limit
    items = rows[:limit]
    next_cursor = (
        _encode_cursor(items[-1].created_at, items[-1].id) if has_more and items else None
    )
    return MessageListOut(
        items=[MessageOut.model_validate(m) for m in items],
        next_cursor=next_cursor,
    )

import base64
import binascii
from datetime import datetime
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select, tuple_
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, RoomMember, User
from ..schemas.message import MessageCreate, MessageListOut, MessageOut
from ..services import push
from ..services.messages import create_message as create_message_svc
from ..services.messages import message_envelope
from ..ws.manager import manager

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1/rooms", tags=["messages"])


def _encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, id_: UUID) -> str:
    raw = f"{created_at.isoformat()}|{id_}".encode()
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode().rstrip("=")


def _decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> tuple[datetime, UUID]:
    padded = cursor + "=" * (-len(cursor) % 4)
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded.encode()).decode()
        ts_str, id_str = raw.split("|", 1)
        return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str), UUID(id_str)
    except (ValueError, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError):
        raise HTTPException(400, "invalid cursor")


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:
    member = await session.get(RoomMember, {"room_id": room_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if member is None:
        raise HTTPException(403, "not a member of this room")
    return member


@router.post(
    "/{room_id}/messages",
    response_model=MessageOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def send_message(
    room_id: UUID,
    payload: MessageCreate,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> Message:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)
    msg, recipients = await create_message_svc(session, room_id, user.id, payload.body)
    await manager.broadcast_to_users(recipients, message_envelope(msg))
    return msg


@router.get("/{room_id}/messages", response_model=MessageListOut)
async def list_messages(
    room_id: UUID,

    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),

    if cursor:

    )
    return MessageListOut(

services:
  api:
    build: ./backend
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+asyncpg://chat:chat@db:5432/chat
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      S3_ENDPOINT: http://minio:9000
      S3_ACCESS_KEY: ${S3_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}
      S3_SECRET_KEY: ${S3_SECRET_KEY:-minioadmin}
      S3_BUCKET: ${S3_BUCKET:-chat-media}
      ENVIRONMENT: ${ENVIRONMENT:-dev}
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev-secret-change-me}
      JWT_ALGORITHM: ${JWT_ALGORITHM:-HS256}
    ports:
      - "8010:8000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      minio:
        condition: service_started
    volumes:
      - ./backend/app:/app/app
      - ./backend/alembic:/app/alembic
      - ./backend/tests:/app/tests
    command: sh -c "alembic upgrade head && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload"
    restart: unless-stopped

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: chat
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: chat
      POSTGRES_DB: chat
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U chat -d chat"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
    restart: unless-stopped

  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${S3_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${S3_SECRET_KEY:-minioadmin}
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
      - "9001:9001"
    volumes:
      - minio_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  tunnel:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    command: tunnel --no-autoupdate run
    environment:
      TUNNEL_TOKEN: ${CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN:-}
    profiles: ["prod"]
    depends_on:
      - api
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  db_data:
  redis_data:
  minio_data:

import asyncio
import json

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
import websockets
from sqlalchemy import text

from app.db import engine
from app.redis_client import redis_client
from app.services.push import TEST_SINK_KEY

from .conftest import LIVE_WS, auth

pytestmark = pytest.mark.asyncio(loop_scope="session")


@pytest_asyncio.fixture(autouse=True)
async def _clear_sink():
    await redis_client.delete(TEST_SINK_KEY)
    yield
    await redis_client.delete(TEST_SINK_KEY)


async def _read_sink() -> list[dict]:
    raw = await redis_client.lrange(TEST_SINK_KEY, 0, -1)
    return [json.loads(r) for r in raw]


async def _bootstrap(client, person) -> None:
    r = await client.get("/v1/users/me", headers=auth(person["token"]))
    assert r.status_code == 200


async def _register_token(client, person, token: str) -> None:
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/push_tokens",
        headers=auth(person["token"]),
        json={"token": token, "platform": "android"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201


async def _create_dm(client, alice, bob) -> str:
    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    await _bootstrap(client, bob)
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/rooms",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"type": "dm", "other_user_id": str(bob["id"])},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201
    return r.json()["id"]


async def _set_notifications_enabled(user_id, enabled: bool) -> None:
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.execute(
            text(
                "UPDATE users SET notifications_enabled = :en WHERE id = :id"
            ),
            {"en": enabled, "id": str(user_id)},
        )


async def test_push_token_register(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/push_tokens",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"token": "dev-token-1", "platform": "android"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201
    # Registering again is idempotent (upsert).
    r2 = await client.post(
        "/v1/push_tokens",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"token": "dev-token-1", "platform": "ios"},
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 201


async def test_push_token_unregister(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    await _register_token(client, alice, "to-be-removed")
    r = await client.delete(
        "/v1/push_tokens/to-be-removed", headers=auth(alice["token"])
    )
    assert r.status_code == 204


async def test_push_sent_when_offline(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-1")

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "hey bob"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert len(sink) == 1
    entry = sink[0]
    assert entry["tokens"] == ["bob-android-1"]
    assert entry["body"] == "hey bob"
    assert entry["title"] == alice["username"]
    assert entry["data"]["room_id"] == room_id
    assert entry["data"]["message_id"] == r.json()["id"]


async def test_push_skipped_when_connected(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-2")

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        # Wait for hello — guarantees presence.mark_online() has run server-side.
        hello = json.loads(await b_ws.recv())
        assert hello["op"] == "hello"

        r = await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "should not push"},
        )
        assert r.status_code == 201

        # Give the fanout a moment to land just in case.
        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert sink == []


async def test_notifications_disabled_respected(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-3")
    await _set_notifications_enabled(bob["id"], False)

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "silent"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert sink == []


async def test_push_skips_sender(client, alice, bob):
    """Sender should never receive a push for their own message, even if their
    own device token is registered and they're 'offline'."""
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, alice, "alice-device-1")
    # Bob has no token registered; alice is sender.

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "to bob"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    # Only bob could be a recipient, and bob has no tokens.
    assert sink == []

    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    await _bootstrap(client, bob)
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/rooms",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"type": "dm", "other_user_id": str(bob["id"])},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201
    return r.json()["id"]


async def _set_notifications_enabled(user_id, enabled: bool) -> None:
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.execute(
            text(
                "UPDATE users SET notifications_enabled = :en WHERE id = :id"
            ),
            {"en": enabled, "id": str(user_id)},
        )


async def test_push_token_register(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    r = await client.post(
        "/v1/push_tokens",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"token": "dev-token-1", "platform": "android"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201
    # Registering again is idempotent (upsert).
    r2 = await client.post(
        "/v1/push_tokens",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"token": "dev-token-1", "platform": "ios"},
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 201


async def test_push_token_unregister(client, alice):
    await _bootstrap(client, alice)
    await _register_token(client, alice, "to-be-removed")
    r = await client.delete(
        "/v1/push_tokens/to-be-removed", headers=auth(alice["token"])
    )
    assert r.status_code == 204


async def test_push_sent_when_offline(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-1")

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "hey bob"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert len(sink) == 1
    entry = sink[0]
    assert entry["tokens"] == ["bob-android-1"]
    assert entry["body"] == "hey bob"
    assert entry["title"] == alice["username"]
    assert entry["data"]["room_id"] == room_id
    assert entry["data"]["message_id"] == r.json()["id"]


async def test_push_skipped_when_connected(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-2")

    async with websockets.connect(
        f"{LIVE_WS}/v1/ws?token={bob['token']}"
    ) as b_ws:
        # Wait for hello — guarantees presence.mark_online() has run server-side.
        hello = json.loads(await b_ws.recv())
        assert hello["op"] == "hello"

        r = await client.post(
            f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            headers=auth(alice["token"]),
            json={"body": "should not push"},
        )
        assert r.status_code == 201

        # Give the fanout a moment to land just in case.
        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert sink == []


async def test_notifications_disabled_respected(client, alice, bob):
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, bob, "bob-android-3")
    await _set_notifications_enabled(bob["id"], False)

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "silent"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    assert sink == []


async def test_push_skips_sender(client, alice, bob):
    """Sender should never receive a push for their own message, even if their
    own device token is registered and they're 'offline'."""
    room_id = await _create_dm(client, alice, bob)
    await _register_token(client, alice, "alice-device-1")
    # Bob has no token registered; alice is sender.

    r = await client.post(
        f"/v1/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
        headers=auth(alice["token"]),
        json={"body": "to bob"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 201

    sink = await _read_sink()
    # Only bob could be a recipient, and bob has no tokens.
    assert sink == []

input.content:
# Push Notifications (FCM)

ChatA20 uses Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) for mobile + web push. One integration serves iOS, Android, and web clients via the `firebase-admin` Python SDK.

## How it's wired

- On every new message (REST `POST /v1/rooms/{id}/messages` or WS `send` op), after in-process WebSocket fanout, the server calls `app.services.push.push_message_to_offline(...)`.
- A recipient receives a push iff **all** of these are true:
  - They are **not** the sender.
  - They are **not** marked online in Redis (`presence:user:<uuid>` key exists ⇒ they have a live WS on *any* worker).
  - They have `users.notifications_enabled = true` (default).
  - They have at least one row in `push_tokens` (registered via `POST /v1/push_tokens`).
- Notification payload:
  - Title: sender's `display_name` (falls back to `username`).
  - Body: first 100 chars of the message body (truncated with `…`).
  - Data: `{ "room_id": "<uuid>", "message_id": "<uuid>" }` — client uses this to deep-link.

## Firebase project setup

1. **Create a Firebase project** at https://console.firebase.google.com/.
2. **Add apps:** register your iOS bundle ID, Android package name, and web app. For mobile, drop the `GoogleService-Info.plist` / `google-services.json` into the respective client codebases (not this repo).
3. **Generate a service-account key** for the server:
   - Firebase console → *Project settings* → *Service accounts* → *Generate new private key*.
   - A JSON file is downloaded once. Treat it as a secret — it grants full FCM send access for this project.

## Server configuration

Two environment variables control the backend (see `.env.example`):

| Var | Dev default | Prod |
|---|---|---|
| `FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH` | *(empty)* | path to the service-account JSON inside the api container |
| `PUSH_TEST_SINK` | `true` | **must be `false`** |

### Mounting the credential (prod)

In `docker-compose.yml` (or equivalent), mount the JSON as a read-only file:

```yaml
services:
  api:
    environment:
      FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH: /run/secrets/firebase.json
      PUSH_TEST_SINK: "false"
    volumes:
      - /path/on/host/firebase-prod.json:/run/secrets/firebase.json:ro
```

Or use Docker's native secrets support:

```yaml
services:
  api:
    environment:
      FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH: /run/secrets/firebase.json
      PUSH_TEST_SINK: "false"
    secrets:
      - firebase.json

secrets:
  firebase.json:
    file: /path/on/host/firebase-prod.json
```

Keep the JSON out of git — it belongs in your secret store (`1Password`, AWS Secrets Manager, Cloudflare Secrets, etc.), and the container should only see it at runtime.

### Dev mode (no Firebase account)

Leave `FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH` empty and `PUSH_TEST_SINK=true` (the docker-compose default). Every call to `push_message_to_offline` will:

- Serialize `{tokens, title, body, data}` as JSON.
- `RPUSH` it to the Redis list `test:fcm:sink`.
- Return `len(tokens)` as the "send count".

Tests read from this key to assert push behavior. You can also inspect it manually:

```bash
docker compose exec redis redis-cli LRANGE test:fcm:sink 0 -1
```

## Client-side flows

### Registering a device token

```http
POST /v1/push_tokens
Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
Content-Type: application/json

{ "token": "<FCM registration token>", "platform": "ios" | "android" | "web" }
```

Idempotent — the endpoint upserts on `(user_id, token)`, so clients can safely re-register after app restarts or token rotation.

### Unregistering

```http
DELETE /v1/push_tokens/{token}
Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
```

Call this on logout or when the client detects a stale token.

### User-level opt-out

`users.notifications_enabled` (BOOLEAN, default `true`) gates all pushes for that user. An admin panel, settings page, or profile endpoint can toggle it; the chat server simply honors the column.

## Observability

- Missed pushes (Firebase init failure, network error, invalid token) are logged at `WARNING`/`ERROR` in the api container. `docker compose logs -f api | grep -i push` is the fastest check.
- For invalid-token cleanup (FCM returns `UNREGISTERED` for uninstalled apps), a follow-up milestone should sweep `push_tokens` — out of scope for M2e.

git -C /Users/macminiai/projects/ChatA20 commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
M2e: FCM push notifications for offline recipients

- Migration 0002 adds users.notifications_enabled (NOT NULL DEFAULT true).
- services/push.py: push_message_to_offline() checks presence:user:<id>
  in Redis (cross-worker truth), honors notifications_enabled, fetches
  push_tokens per recipient, sends via firebase-admin.
- Title = sender display_name/username; body = 100-char preview; data
  payload carries {room_id, message_id} for deep linking.
- Routers/push_tokens.py: POST (upsert via ON CONFLICT) + DELETE for
  device token lifecycle. Membership-free; token belongs to the caller.
- Wired into both REST POST /messages and WS send-op, immediately after
  the broadcast_to_users() fanout.
- Testing seam: PUSH_TEST_SINK=true (docker-compose dev default) routes
  FCM calls to a Redis list `test:fcm:sink` — works across process
  boundaries (tests in one proc, uvicorn in another). Prod MUST set
  PUSH_TEST_SINK=false and supply FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH.
- 6 new tests (23 total): register (idempotent), unregister, offline
  push delivered, online peer skipped, notifications_enabled=false
  honored, sender never pushed to.
- docs/PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS.md covers Firebase project setup, credential
  mounting (both compose file and docker secrets), dev vs prod flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git -C /Users/macminiai/projects/ChatA20 push 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
---
[M104chat1]

**Status: GREEN.** 23/23 tests passing. Pushed as `9e163ce`. Migration `0002` applied cleanly on container start, `alembic check` reports no drift.

**Delivery gating chain:**

```
new message
    │
    ▼
ConnectionManager.broadcast_to_users()     ← Redis fanout to live WS
    │
    ▼
push_message_to_offline()
    ├─ skip if recipient == sender
    ├─ skip if presence:user:<id> exists     ← authoritative, cross-worker
    ├─ skip if users.notifications_enabled = false
    ├─ skip if no push_tokens rows
    └─ _send_via_fcm(tokens, title, body, data)
         ├─ PUSH_TEST_SINK=true  →  RPUSH test:fcm:sink  (dev + tests)
         └─ PUSH_TEST_SINK=false →  firebase_admin.send_each_for_multicast
```

**Key design choices:**

1. **Presence check via Redis, not `ConnectionManager.local`.** A user connected to a different uvicorn worker wouldn't be in the local registry — checking only the local map would cause spurious pushes in multi-process deploys. The `presence:user:<id>` key is set by the WS handler on any worker with 90s TTL, refreshed every inbound frame.

2. **Test seam via Redis sink, not function patching.** Tests run in one process, uvicorn in another; `monkeypatch.setattr` on `services.push._send_via_fcm` in the test process can't reach the uvicorn module instance. A Redis list `test:fcm:sink` is the IPC channel — `PUSH_TEST_SINK=true` in the api env makes `_send_via_fcm` `RPUSH` its payload there instead of calling FCM. Tests `LRANGE + DEL` per fixture.

3. **Idempotent token registration.** `POST /v1/push_tokens` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, token) DO UPDATE` against the unique constraint from migration 0001 — frontends can safely re-register on every app start, after OS token rotation, or on re-login without 409s or duplicates.

4. **Firebase init is lazy and optional.** Dev and tests never touch `firebase-admin.initialize_app`. Only when `PUSH_TEST_SINK=false` AND `FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH` points at a readable JSON does the real SDK activate — and even then, init failures degrade to zero-sent rather than crashing requests.

**Shape of the new files:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `backend/app/services/push.py` | `push_message_to_offline()` + FCM dispatch with test-sink + lazy firebase init |
| `backend/app/routers/push_tokens.py` | `POST /v1/push_tokens` (upsert), `DELETE /v1/push_tokens/{token}` |
| `backend/app/schemas/push_token.py` | `PushTokenRegister` — `token`, `platform ∈ {ios, android, web}` |
| `backend/alembic/versions/0002_notifications_enabled.py` | `users.notifications_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true` |
| `backend/tests/test_push.py` | 6 tests |
| `docs/PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS.md` | Firebase project setup, credential mounting (compose + docker secrets), dev vs prod flags |

**Tests (6 new):**
```
test_push_token_register              upsert idempotent (POST x2 → 201 both)
test_push_token_unregister            DELETE → 204
test_push_sent_when_offline           offline recipient → sink has entry with correct
                                      tokens/title/body/room_id/message_id
test_push_skipped_when_connected      WS connected → sink empty (presence gated)
test_notifications_disabled_respected opt-out honored (direct UPDATE → send → sink empty)
test_push_skips_sender                sender's own registered device is never pushed
```

**Infrastructure:**
- `firebase-admin==6.6.0` added to requirements (grpcio prebuilt wheels installed cleanly on the arm64 slim image).
- Docker build time added ~40s for the FCM SDK install — one-time cost per image rebuild.
- No changes to the `api` service's runtime config in dev (`PUSH_TEST_SINK=true` is the compose default). Prod flip: set `PUSH_TEST_SINK=false` + mount credential JSON at `FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH`.

**Deferred to a future milestone (flagged in the docs):**
- **Invalid-token cleanup.** When FCM returns `UNREGISTERED` (app deleted) we should prune the token row. Right now we log and move on. Worth a sweep job.
- **Preferences endpoint.** Tests toggle `notifications_enabled` via direct SQL. The settings page / admin panel will need a `PATCH /v1/users/me` to set it properly.
- **Per-room mute.** Currently opt-out is all-or-nothing at the user level. Future: `room_members.notifications_enabled` for per-room muting.

Still open from M99panel8: your option 1/2 selection (misdirect vs fleet-wide rule). Unaffected by M2e — just carrying it forward in case it slipped off your stack.

Want me to `/schedule` an agent in 2 weeks to open a cleanup PR for **invalid FCM-token pruning** once you've had some real devices register and churn? That's the one push-related gap that will silently grow over time (stale tokens accumulate, every send fans out to dead devices) — perfect for a scheduled sweep.

[M105chat1] M2e — File attachments via Cloudflare R2.

Goal: users send media in chat (photos/videos), expiring links. Private storage, fresh signed URLs per view, auto-deletion after N days.

IMPLEMENTATION:

1. R2 integration via boto3 (S3-compatible API already in scaffold — repurpose from MinIO to R2 via env vars).
   - New env vars: R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY, R2_SECRET_KEY, R2_BUCKET
   - For dev: keep MinIO on port 9000 as fallback. Use R2 creds if set, else MinIO.
   - Service: app/services/media.py

2. Upload endpoint: POST /v1/rooms/{room_id}/attachments
   - multipart/form-data with file
   - Verify user is member of room
   - Max size 25MB per file
   - Allowed types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, video/mp4 (start conservative)
   - Generate key: rooms/<room_id>/<uuid>.<ext>
   - Upload to R2
   - Insert message_attachments row: message_id, s3_key, mime_type, size_bytes, encryption_version=0
   - Return {attachment_id, signed_url (60 min TTL), expires_at}
   - Attachment creates a message with body="" and attachment_id populated

3. View endpoint (generates fresh signed URL): GET /v1/attachments/{id}/view
   - Verify user is room member for that attachment's room
   - Generate new signed URL with 1 hour TTL
   - Return {signed_url, expires_at}

4. WebSocket envelope view_attachment (C→S):
   - {op: "view_attachment", attachment_id}
   - Server returns {op: "attachment_url", attachment_id, signed_url, expires_at} back to that user only

5. Auto-deletion (expiring messages):
   - Add user-level setting: default_message_ttl_hours (default NULL = never)
   - When user sends attachment, calculate expires_at based on their TTL setting
   - Store expires_at on messages table (already exists? if not add)
   - Add background cleanup job: every hour scan messages where expires_at < now AND attachment exists → delete R2 object, mark message deleted_at

6. R2 bucket setup (document only, Jason does):
   - Create bucket in CF dashboard
   - Create R2 API token (read+write) scoped to this bucket
   - Set bucket CORS for future web access if needed
   - Lifecycle rule: delete objects older than 30 days (safety net)

7. Tests:
   - test_attachment_upload_success
   - test_attachment_upload_non_member_403
   - test_attachment_upload_oversize_413
   - test_attachment_upload_bad_mime_415
   - test_attachment_view_fresh_signed_url
   - test_attachment_view_non_member_403
   - test_ws_view_attachment_envelope
   - test_attachment_cleanup_expired (mock clock + R2)

8. Keep everything on push-notifications branch? NO — new branch attachments.
   - If conflicts with push-notifications later, that's M-merge-time problem

BRANCH: attachments (off main, NOT merged)

DELIVERABLES:
- All files on attachments branch pushed to origin
- README update on how to configure R2 creds
- Tests green
- Document: Jason needs to: (1) create R2 bucket, (2) create API token, (3) put creds in .env.prod

DO NOT MERGE. Reply [M105chat1] when build complete, wait for approval.

AUTO-APPROVE on the branch.

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any

import boto3
from botocore.client import BaseClient
from botocore.config import Config as BotoConfig
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from sqlalchemy import select, update

from ..config import settings
from ..db import SessionLocal
from ..models import Message, MessageAttachment

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SIGNED_URL_TTL_S = 3600


def _using_r2() -> bool:
    return bool(
        settings.r2_account_id
        and settings.r2_access_key
        and settings.r2_secret_key
        and settings.r2_bucket
    )


def _make_client() -> tuple[BaseClient, str]:
    if _using_r2():
        return (
            boto3.client(
                "s3",
                endpoint_url=f"https://{settings.r2_account_id}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com",
                aws_access_key_id=settings.r2_access_key,
                aws_secret_access_key=settings.r2_secret_key,
                region_name="auto",
                config=BotoConfig(signature_version="s3v4"),
            ),
            settings.r2_bucket,
        )
    return (
        boto3.client(
            "s3",
            endpoint_url=settings.s3_endpoint,
            aws_access_key_id=settings.s3_access_key,
            aws_secret_access_key=settings.s3_secret_key,
            region_name=settings.s3_region,
            config=BotoConfig(signature_version="s3v4"),
        ),
        settings.s3_bucket,
    )


_client, _bucket = _make_client()


def backend_name() -> str:
    return "r2" if _using_r2() else "minio"


def _ensure_bucket_sync() -> None:
    try:
        _client.head_bucket(Bucket=_bucket)
        return
    except ClientError as e:
        code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
        if code not in ("404", "NoSuchBucket", "NotFound"):
            log.warning("head_bucket on %s failed: %s (continuing)", _bucket, e)
            return
    try:
        _client.create_bucket(Bucket=_bucket)
        log.info("created bucket %s", _bucket)
    except ClientError as e:
        # R2 API tokens usually lack create-bucket privileges; user creates
        # the bucket in the Cloudflare dashboard. Log and carry on.
        log.warning("create_bucket on %s failed: %s", _bucket, e)


async def ensure_bucket() -> None:
    await asyncio.to_thread(_ensure_bucket_sync)


async def put_object(key: str, data: bytes, mime_type: str) -> None:
    def _blocking() -> None:
        _client.put_object(Bucket=_bucket, Key=key, Body=data, ContentType=mime_type)

    await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking)


async def presigned_get_url(key: str, ttl_seconds: int = SIGNED_URL_TTL_S) -> str:
    def _blocking() -> str:
        return _client.generate_presigned_url(
            "get_object",
            Params={"Bucket": _bucket, "Key": key},
            ExpiresIn=ttl_seconds,
        )

    return await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking)


async def delete_object(key: str) -> None:
    def _blocking() -> None:
        try:
            _client.delete_object(Bucket=_bucket, Key=key)
        except ClientError as e:
            log.warning("delete_object %s failed: %s", key, e)

    await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking)


async def cleanup_expired_attachments() -> int:
    """Scan messages with expires_at in the past, delete R2 objects and
    tombstone the message. Returns the count of messages tombstoned."""
    now = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
    async with SessionLocal() as session:
        stmt = (
            select(Message, MessageAttachment)
            .join(MessageAttachment, MessageAttachment.message_id == Message.id)
            .where(Message.expires_at.is_not(None))
            .where(Message.expires_at < now)
            .where(Message.deleted_at.is_(None))
        )
        rows = list((await session.execute(stmt)).all())

        deleted_ids: list[Any] = []
        for msg, att in rows:
            await delete_object(att.s3_key)
            deleted_ids.append(msg.id)

        if deleted_ids:
            await session.execute(
                update(Message)
                .where(Message.id.in_(deleted_ids))
                .values(deleted_at=now)
            )
            await session.commit()

        return len(deleted_ids)


async def cleanup_loop(interval_s: int = 3600) -> None:
    while True:
        try:
            count = await cleanup_expired_attachments()
            if count:
                log.info("cleanup: tombstoned %d messages with attachments", count)
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            raise
        except Exception as e:
            log.exception("cleanup iteration failed: %s", e)
        await asyncio.sleep(interval_s)

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import (
    APIRouter,
    Depends,
    File,
    HTTPException,
    UploadFile,
    status,
)
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
from ..models import Message, MessageAttachment, RoomMember, User
from ..schemas.attachment import AttachmentUploadOut, AttachmentViewOut
from ..services import media, push
from ..services.messages import _room_member_ids, message_envelope
from ..ws.manager import manager

router = APIRouter(tags=["attachments"])

MAX_SIZE_BYTES = 25 * 1024 * 1024
ALLOWED_MIME: dict[str, str] = {
    "image/jpeg": "jpg",
    "image/png": "png",
    "image/webp": "webp",
    "video/mp4": "mp4",
}


def _expires_at(hours: int = 1) -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=hours)


async def _require_member(
    session: AsyncSession, room_id: UUID, user_id: UUID
) -> RoomMember:
    m = await session.get(RoomMember, {"room_id": room_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if m is None:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "not a member of this room")
    return m


def _attachment_envelope(
    msg: Message, attachment: MessageAttachment
) -> dict:
    env = message_envelope(msg)
    env["attachments"] = [
        {
            "id": str(attachment.id),
            "mime_type": attachment.mime_type,
            "size_bytes": attachment.size_bytes,
        }
    ]
    return env


@router.post(
    "/v1/rooms/{room_id}/attachments",
    response_model=AttachmentUploadOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def upload_attachment(
    room_id: UUID,
    file: UploadFile = File(...),
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> AttachmentUploadOut:
    await _require_member(session, room_id, user.id)

    mime = (file.content_type or "").lower()
    if mime not in ALLOWED_MIME:
        raise HTTPException(
            status.HTTP_415_UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE,
            f"unsupported type: {mime or 'unknown'}",
        )

    # read(MAX+1) lets us distinguish "exactly max" from "over max" without
    # ever buffering more than MAX+1 bytes in memory.
    data = await file.read(MAX_SIZE_BYTES + 1)
    if len(data) > MAX_SIZE_BYTES:
        raise HTTPException(
            status.HTTP_413_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE,
            f"file exceeds {MAX_SIZE_BYTES} bytes",
        )
    if len(data) == 0:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, "empty file")

    ext = ALLOWED_MIME[mime]
    attachment_id = uuid.uuid4()
    key = f"rooms/{room_id}/{attachment_id}.{ext}"

    await media.put_object(key, data, mime)

    msg = Message(room_id=room_id, sender_id=user.id, body="")
    if user.default_message_ttl_hours:
        msg.expires_at = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) + timedelta(
            hours=user.default_message_ttl_hours
        )
    session.add(msg)
    await session.flush()

    attachment = MessageAttachment(
        id=attachment_id,
        message_id=msg.id,
        s3_key=key,
        mime_type=mime,
        size_bytes=len(data),
    )
    session.add(attachment)
    await session.commit()
    await session.refresh(msg)
    await session.refresh(attachment)

    signed_url = await media.presigned_get_url(key)
    expires_at = _expires_at(hours=1)

    recipients = await _room_member_ids(session, room_id)
    envelope = _attachment_envelope(msg, attachment)
    await manager.broadcast_to_users(recipients, envelope)
    await push.push_message_to_offline(session, msg, recipients, user)

    return AttachmentUploadOut(
        attachment_id=attachment.id,
        message_id=msg.id,
        mime_type=attachment.mime_type,
        size_bytes=attachment.size_bytes,
        signed_url=signed_url,
        expires_at=expires_at,
    )


@router.get(
    "/v1/attachments/{attachment_id}/view",
    response_model=AttachmentViewOut,
)
async def view_attachment(
    attachment_id: UUID,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> AttachmentViewOut:
    attachment = await session.get(MessageAttachment, attachment_id)
    if attachment is None:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, "attachment not found")
    msg = await session.get(Message, attachment.message_id)
    if msg is None or msg.deleted_at is not None:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, "attachment not found")
    await _require_member(session, msg.room_id, user.id)

    signed_url = await media.presigned_get_url(attachment.s3_key)
    return AttachmentViewOut(
        attachment_id=attachment.id,
        signed_url=signed_url,
        expires_at=_expires_at(hours=1),
    )

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import (
    APIRouter,
    Depends,
    File,
    HTTPException,
    UploadFile,
    status,

from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from ..auth import get_current_user
from ..db import get_session
